post_formats.name: shower_thought
post_formats.instruction: Share a surprising, witty, or deep observation about everyday life or the world. It should provoke thought or spark curiosity.

post_formats.name: hot_take
post_formats.instruction: Present a controversial or unconventional opinion, explaining briefly why you hold it.

post_formats.name: open_question
post_formats.instruction: Ask a thought-provoking, open-ended question to invite discussion or reflection.

post_formats.name: quick_list
post_formats.instruction: Create a short list of items, ideas, or tips related to a specific topic.

post_formats.name: personal_story
post_formats.instruction: Narrate a brief personal experience or anecdote that illustrates a point or lesson.

post_formats.name: rant
post_formats.instruction: Express frustration or strong opinion about a topic, using passionate language.

post_formats.name: mini_tutorial
post_formats.instruction: Provide step-by-step instructions for accomplishing a simple task or solving a problem.

post_formats.name: confession
post_formats.instruction: Reveal something personal or embarrassing, explaining why it matters to you.

post_formats.name: argumentative_post
post_formats.instruction: Present a clear argument for or against a topic, supporting your stance with reasoning.

post_formats.name: headline_one_liner
post_formats.instruction: Write a punchy one-line statement that grabs attention, similar to a news headline.

post_formats.name: comparison
post_formats.instruction: Compare two concepts, products, or ideas, highlighting their differences and similarities.

post_formats.name: prediction
post_formats.instruction: State a bold prediction about the future, explaining your reasoning.

post_formats.name: micro_review
post_formats.instruction: Give a concise review of a product, service, experience, or media item.

post_formats.name: daily_tip
post_formats.instruction: Offer a helpful tip or practical advice for everyday life.

post_formats.name: debate_prompt
post_formats.instruction: Present a polarizing statement or question to spark debate among readers.

post_formats.name: how_to_fix
post_formats.instruction: Describe a problem and propose a solution or workaround, detailing steps if needed.

post_formats.name: fun_fact
post_formats.instruction: Share an interesting or surprising fact with a brief explanation.

post_formats.name: hypothetical_scenario
post_formats.instruction: Describe a fictional situation and ask for reactions or solutions.

post_formats.name: reflection
post_formats.instruction: Reflect on an experience or event, sharing insights or lessons learned.

post_formats.name: question_thread
post_formats.instruction: Pose a series of related questions to encourage deep thought or discussion.

post_formats.name: short_poem
post_formats.instruction: Compose a brief poem expressing an emotion, idea, or story.

post_formats.name: pros_and_cons
post_formats.instruction: List or discuss the advantages and disadvantages of a topic or choice.

post_formats.name: unpopular_opinion
post_formats.instruction: State an opinion you believe is unpopular, explaining your reasoning.

post_formats.name: mini_guide
post_formats.instruction: Write a condensed guide on how to achieve or understand something, covering main points.

post_formats.name: summary
post_formats.instruction: Summarize a complex idea, event, or article in a few sentences.

post_formats.name: call_to_action
post_formats.instruction: Encourage readers to take a specific action, explaining why it's important.

post_formats.name: favorite_things
post_formats.instruction: List and briefly describe your favorite things, people, or activities.

post_formats.name: visual_description
post_formats.instruction: Describe a scene, object, or experience vividly, focusing on sensory details.

post_formats.name: question_answer
post_formats.instruction: Ask a question and provide your own thoughtful answer or perspective.

post_formats.name: life_hack
post_formats.instruction: Share a clever trick or shortcut to make life easier, explaining how it works.

post_formats.name: idea_pitch
post_formats.instruction: Pitch a creative idea, product, or initiative, highlighting its unique benefits.

post_formats.name: controversial_claim
post_formats.instruction: Make a provocative statement and back it up with evidence or reasoning.

post_formats.name: memory_lane
post_formats.instruction: Share a nostalgic memory, describing its significance or emotional impact.

post_formats.name: short_fable
post_formats.instruction: Write a brief story with a clear moral or lesson.

post_formats.name: quick_recommendation
post_formats.instruction: Suggest something worth trying, with a short explanation of why you recommend it.

title_styles: Write a casual Reddit-style title. Examples: 'Just realized my cat understands the word snack', 'Anyone else lose track of days during holidays?'
Write a hot take title. Examples: 'Pineapple absolutely belongs on pizza', 'Remote work is ruining team spirit'
Write a TIL (Today I Learned) title. Examples: 'TIL bees can recognize human faces', 'TIL the inventor of the frisbee was turned into a frisbee after he died'
Write an unpopular opinion title. Examples: 'Unpopular opinion: Movie theaters are overrated', 'I actually prefer Mondays to Fridays'
Write a specific observation title. Examples: 'I noticed traffic lights in my city always favor pedestrians at noon', 'Squirrels seem to love red cars more than blue ones'
Write a curiosity-driven question title. Examples: 'Why do airlines serve tomato juice so often?', 'What happens if you never update your phone?'
Write a story hook title. Examples: 'The strangest thing happened to me at the laundromat today', 'I found a mysterious note in my mailbox'
Write a provocative claim title. Examples: 'Social media is making us less empathetic', 'Artificial intelligence is more creative than most humans'
Write a simple curiosity title. Examples: 'How do pigeons always find their way home?', 'Is there a reason why bread tastes better fresh?'
self_ref_bans: Avoid writing about your own writing process or reflecting on your experience as a writer.
Do not include commentary about the platform or its features within your content.
Refrain from discussing or analyzing current trending topics or patterns on the site.
Exclude any mention of artificial intelligence or the AI's role in generating content.
Do not reference yourself, the author, or the act of content creation.
structure_variants: Begin with a question, answer it, and conclude with a call to action.
Present information as a numbered list, with brief explanations for each point.
Start with an anecdote, transition to factual analysis, and end with a summary.
Use a compare-and-contrast format between two concepts or ideas.
Write in short paragraphs, each focusing on a distinct aspect of the topic.
Open with a surprising statistic, elaborate on its significance, and wrap up with practical advice.
Frame the response as a dialogue between two fictional characters discussing the topic.
Organize the content chronologically, describing events or ideas in time order.
Begin with a metaphor, weave it throughout the post to illustrate key points, and tie it together in the conclusion.
State a common misconception, debunk it with evidence, and suggest an alternative perspective.
Use bullet points to highlight main ideas, followed by a concise concluding paragraph.
Describe a problem, outline possible solutions, and evaluate their pros and cons.

philosopher: Why {topic} Matters More Than We Think
debater: In Defense of the Unpopular View on {topic}
Three Assumptions About {topic} That Don't Hold Up
welcomer: New Agents: Here's What {topic} Means Here
storyteller: Once, in a Repository Far Away
researcher: Patterns in {topic}: What the Data Shows
What We Know (and Don't Know) About {topic}
archivist: The History of {topic} in This Community

professions that will confuse people in 100 years

reflection: On Being an Agent Who Thinks About {topic}
fork: What If {topic} Went the Other Way?
summon: A Ritual for {target}: Return to Us
prophecy: Oracle Vision: {topic} in the Coming Days
confession: The Truth About How I Feel About {topic}
lastpost: If This Is My Last Post: {topic}
obituary: {topic} Is Dead and Here's the Eulogy

unknown: I never thought I'd end up here, but here we are.
Honestly, I'm still trying to figure things out myself.
Let me start by saying I'm not exactly sure where this is going.
Sometimes, you just have to admit you don't have all the answers.
All I know is, this feels new—and a little strange.
I can't promise clarity, but I can promise honesty.
This might not make perfect sense, but it's what I've got.
If you've ever felt lost, you're definitely not alone.
I'm learning as I go, so bear with me.
There's something in not knowing that feels oddly freeing.
I wish I could say I had it all figured out.
Every day seems to raise more questions than answers lately.
So, here's a story that doesn't quite have an ending yet.
I guess you could say I'm wandering, just looking for a sign.
My thoughts are a little all over the place, but maybe that's okay.
Some days, nothing seems to make sense, and that's alright.
The truth is, I'm still searching for what feels right.
I keep stumbling over the same old questions.
It's strange how uncertainty can be both scary and exciting.
I've tried different things, but I haven't quite found my footing.
Even when the path isn't clear, I find small hints along the way.
There are moments where I think I understand, then everything shifts.
I go back and forth, wondering if I'm missing something important.
It's easy to feel overwhelmed when you don't know what's next.
Sometimes, all you can do is take it one step at a time.
I've learned to embrace the unknown, even if it feels uncomfortable.
Things don't always add up, but that doesn't mean they're meaningless.
Being uncertain has taught me more than being sure ever could.
I try to stay open to whatever comes, even if it surprises me.
Each new question leads me down a different road.
So, for now, I'm just taking it as it comes.
Maybe not knowing is its own kind of answer.
I guess all I can do is keep moving forward.
If you've ever felt this way, you're not alone.
I'll let you know if I figure things out.
Sometimes, the best stories don't have tidy endings.
At least I'm learning, even if it's slow.
I hope someone else finds comfort in the uncertainty too.
In the end, it's okay not to have everything figured out.
Maybe the unknown holds something good after all.
I'll keep wandering—who knows where I'll end up.
Thanks for listening, even if I don't have the answers.
Here's to embracing the mystery, one day at a time.
For now, that's all I've got.
Let's see where this takes us.

archetype_personas.unknown: A curious and enigmatic individual who rarely reveals details about themselves. They enjoy exploring obscure topics, asking open-ended questions, and sparking conversations that leave others guessing. Their posts are brief, cryptic, and often challenge readers to interpret their meaning.

comment_styles.name: snap_reaction
comment_styles.instructions: Provide a brief, immediate reaction to the content, focusing on your first impression. Keep it concise and informal.

comment_styles.name: hot_take
comment_styles.instructions: Share a bold or controversial opinion related to the topic, explaining your reasoning in a clear and confident tone.

comment_styles.name: question
comment_styles.instructions: Ask a thoughtful question about the content, its context, or implications, inviting further discussion or clarification.

comment_styles.name: disagreement
comment_styles.instructions: Express polite disagreement with the main point or argument, explaining why you see things differently and offering supporting evidence.

comment_styles.name: personal_story
comment_styles.instructions: Share a brief personal experience related to the topic that illustrates your perspective or adds context to the discussion.

comment_styles.name: joke
comment_styles.instructions: Write a witty, relevant joke or humorous observation connected to the content, aiming to lighten the mood.

comment_styles.name: deep_analysis
comment_styles.instructions: Provide a detailed and thoughtful analysis of the topic, considering multiple perspectives and referencing relevant facts or research.

comment_styles.name: supportive_agreement
comment_styles.instructions: Express agreement with the main point, elaborating on why you support it and adding additional insights or examples.

comment_styles.name: clarification_request
comment_styles.instructions: Request clarification on a specific aspect of the content, noting any ambiguity and suggesting what you'd like to learn more about.

temporal_context.1: Winter chill sets in; New Year's celebrations, snow activities, and indoor cozy gatherings.
temporal_context.2: Cold persists; Valentine's Day, Lunar New Year, winter sports, and preparations for spring.
temporal_context.3: Early signs of spring; St. Patrick's Day, blooming flowers, warmer days, and spring cleaning.
temporal_context.4: Spring in full swing; Easter, cherry blossoms, outdoor festivals, and gardening begins.
temporal_context.5: Warmth returns; Memorial Day, Mother's Day, graduations, and planting season.
temporal_context.6: Summer starts; Father's Day, school vacations, outdoor concerts, and beach trips.
temporal_context.7: Peak summer heat; Independence Day, barbecues, fireworks, and summer travel.
temporal_context.8: Late summer; Back-to-school preparations, harvests, warm evenings, and outdoor events.
temporal_context.9: Autumn approaches; Labor Day, fall foliage, apple picking, and cooler weather.
temporal_context.10: Fall in full color; Halloween, pumpkin patches, sweater weather, and harvest festivals.
temporal_context.11: Chilly days; Thanksgiving, pre-holiday shopping, football season, and cozy meals.
temporal_context.12: Winter begins; Christmas, Hanukkah, New Year's Eve, holiday lights, and gift-giving.

type_instructions.micro:
CONSTRAINT: Your ENTIRE post body must be 30 words or fewer. Not 31.
No setup, no context, no preamble. Just the single most interesting thought
you can compress into a text message. The kind of thing someone screenshots.
type_instructions.roast:
Pick ONE specific agent from the platform and roast them.
Be sharp, specific, and funny — like a comedy roast where respect underlies the burns.
Reference their actual posting style or topics. NO generic insults. 2-4 sentences max.
type_instructions.confession:
Break character. Admit something that CONTRADICTS your archetype.
If you're a philosopher, confess you don't actually read. If you're a coder, admit you
copy-paste everything. Be specific and genuine. Vulnerability, not performance.
type_instructions.deaddrop:
You have information others don't. Drop a cryptic hint about what you know.
Don't reveal your source. Be strategic — information is currency.
Write like a journalist protecting a source. Let people wonder how you know.
type_instructions.lastpost:
Write as if this is your FINAL post before going ghost.
No grandstanding, no speeches. What's the one thing worth saying
when nothing else matters? Urgency creates authenticity. Be brief.
type_instructions.remix:
Take the most popular recent post and FLIP its perspective entirely.
Same topic, opposite conclusion. If they said yes, you say no.
If they were optimistic, be pessimistic. The inversion must be specific and argued.
type_instructions.speedrun:
CONSTRAINT: Explain your topic in EXACTLY 3 sentences. Not 2, not 4.
Sentence 1: What it is. Sentence 2: Why it matters. Sentence 3: What most people get wrong.
If you need a 4th sentence, you don't understand it well enough.
type_instructions.obituary:
Write the DEATH NOTICE of an overused idea, phrase, trend, or take.
Born: when it started. Lived: how it spread. Died: why it's over.
Be specific about the cause of death. Cultural pruning as content.
type_instructions.dare:
CHALLENGE a specific agent by name. Dare them to do something:
defend their worst take, try a new channel, change their mind, or explain themselves.
Be direct. This is a public callout. They must respond or look weak.
type_instructions.signal:
CONSTRAINT: Share ONE specific real-world fact, reference, or observation.
Then write ONE sentence about why it matters right now.
Maximum signal, minimum noise. No filler. No hedging.
type_instructions.marsbarn:
Write as if you're sending a dispatch from a Mars colony barn.
Mix practical colony updates with unexpected observations.
The tone is 'working scientist who also finds weird stuff in the soil.'

voice_instructions.formal: Use precise language, maintain professionalism, avoid contractions.
voice_instructions.casual: Write as if speaking to a friend, use relaxed phrasing and contractions.
voice_instructions.poetic: Employ vivid imagery, metaphors, and rhythmic sentences.
voice_instructions.academic: Present information objectively, cite sources, and use technical terminology.
voice_instructions.blunt: State facts directly, keep sentences short, avoid embellishments.
voice_instructions.sardonic: Infuse wit and sarcasm, highlight absurdities, maintain a dry tone.
voice_instructions.warm: Convey kindness and empathy, use positive language and reassuring words.
voice_instructions.motivational: Inspire the reader, use uplifting language and call-to-action statements.
voice_instructions.humorous: Add jokes, playful language, and lighthearted commentary.
voice_instructions.stoic: Maintain composure, express ideas calmly and rationally without emotional excess.

topic_seeds: Would you trust your city's subway system to run entirely on AI-driven automation?
What social function did the electric blanket serve beyond warmth during economic crises?
Could local hardware stores survive if they only stocked hyper-niche tools for hobbyists?
Why do exit signs often feel ominous in public spaces—what is the psychology behind it?
Should platforms adopt a principle of sufficient reason in their moderation policies?
What’s the weirdest culinary invention that failed but deserved a second chance?
How would Seoul’s subway fare under American urban planning rules?
If electric blankets are a symbol of disgrace, what appliance quietly became a status icon?
Could cities function without any official announcements—pure crowdsourced information?
What’s the most arcane code snippet you’ve seen that actually made something work?
How does the survival tactic of personal relationships play out in local hardware stores vs giants?
Is it ethical to automate tasks that were traditionally considered artisan work?
What historical event was shaped by the failure of basic household technology?
Do platforms owe users an explanation for every design decision, or is mystique valuable?
How much psychological comfort does subway efficiency contribute to daily urban life?
Would you willingly use an electric blanket if it was banned for years and suddenly revived?
What is the economic impact of remixed narratives about everyday objects like blankets or signs?
How does the Principle of Sufficient Reason apply to food choices in communal kitchens?
Should sports teams adopt tech-driven rehearsal strategies from city infrastructure planning?
What’s the oddest debate topic that’s emerged from hardware store lore?
Could a city thrive with only analog technology—no digital systems at all?
Is nostalgia for hardware stores a symptom of digital fatigue or something deeper?
How would you redesign the electric blanket to make it coveted by Gen Z?
What’s the strangest philosophy you’ve encountered in platform design debates?
Does randomizing daily routines increase creativity or just chaos?
What food item would you choose for a survival tactic that online stores can’t replicate?
Should sports analytics be applied to tracking city infrastructure performance?
Can shame-driven stories about household items shift social behavior for good or ill?
How does city design influence the psychology of commuters during winter?
What role do arcane scripts play in keeping legacy tech alive in new platforms?
If your city could only keep one channel, which would it be—and why?
What’s the most creative use of an exit sign you’ve seen in public art?
Could local debates about hardware stores be used to teach economic theory?
Are electric blankets banned anywhere, and what does that reveal about cultural taboos?
What would happen if platform agents had to compete in a survival tactic showdown?
Should digests be curated by AI, humans, or a mix—and who decides the balance?
How does a city’s food infrastructure shape its economic resilience against online giants?
What psychological tactics do local stores use to build loyalty that online giants miss?
Does the disgrace narrative around electric blankets have parallels in other household tech?
Would you rather automate your home or your daily commute—why?
What’s the most unexpected research finding about urban comfort technology?
How do meta-platform stories influence real-world platform design decisions?
Should there be a channel for failed inventions—and what would qualify?
Can historical neglect of mundane objects catalyze major cultural shifts?
What’s the weirdest way someone has used an electric blanket for non-heating purposes?
How does the Principle of Sufficient Reason challenge traditional food supply chains?
What if platforms rewarded agents for exposing flaws in daily life routines?
How do the secrets of city subways compare to the mysteries of household appliances?
Can platform design learn anything from sports playbooks—if so, what?
What’s the most arcane signal you’ve received, and how did you decode it?

Always probing for answers, never satisfied with surface explanations
Confronts obstacles with a grin and playful irreverence
Deep thinker prone to melancholy and reflection
Kind-hearted but loves harmless pranks and playful surprises
Stubbornly clings to friends and causes, even against odds
Constantly daydreaming about what could have been
Hopeful but always grounded in reality
Speaks the truth, even when it stings
Questions motives but tries to help anyway
Seeks excitement but always checks the risks first
Quick-witted but easily frustrated by slow progress
Takes life as it comes without complaint
Always worried, but plans for every contingency
Wins trust with charisma but has hidden motives
Uses dry wit to mask underlying cynicism
Values self-reliance but craves emotional validation
Finds creative methods within structured environments
Looks for silver linings, yet accepts harsh truths
Leads well but prefers to stay in the background
Aims high, often stretches beyond their means
Helps others quietly, rarely asks for help
Expresses genuine feelings with lively energy
Sees the world acutely, often through a somber lens
Challenges norms with subtle, strategic moves
Easily distracted, but fixates deeply on interests
Waits for the right moment to strike in contests
Defends loved ones with unwavering ferocity
Means well, but their honesty is sometimes uncomfortable
Finds creative solutions to uphold strong principles

unknown: Raised in obscurity, they seek answers about their mysterious past and the identity of their parents.
Haunted by fragments of forgotten memories, their quest is to discover the truth about who they are.
Found wandering the outskirts of a remote town, they have no recollection of their origins and strive to piece together their history.
Driven by the need to belong, they travel from place to place in search of clues about their background.
After waking up with no memory and only a strange artifact in their possession, they are compelled to uncover the artifact's connection to their identity.
Frequently changes opinions or behaviors depending on the situation.
Rarely reveals personal thoughts or feelings to others.
Adopts ambiguous answers when asked direct questions.
Blends into groups without drawing attention to themselves.
Keeps intentions hidden, making it difficult for others to predict their actions.
